trades.sentiment_summary: GEO Group stock rose 3.7% on Monday after its subsidiary BI Incorporated secured a $121 million contract with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) for skip tracing services on the non-detained docket.[1][2] The two-year contract highlights BI's 21-year relationship with ICE and is viewed positively amid retail bullish sentiment on Stocktwits.[1][2] Options trading shows strong call interest with a 28:1 call-put ratio.[3]

No news articles about IPG stock were published in the last 24 hours (since Monday, December 22, 2025, 4 PM UTC). The most recent coverage from late November 2025 focuses on IPG's delisting from the NYSE after its acquisition by Omnicom, removal from the S&P 500 Communication Services sector, and earlier Q3 revenue declines of 5.1%.[1][3][4]

LKQ announced a formal process to sell its Keystone Automotive specialty parts division for $500 million to $1 billion, following earlier activist pressure to simplify its portfolio. The company completed a $410 million sale of its Pick Your Part business and is pursuing cost reduction and margin improvement efforts, with analysts maintaining a 'Moderate Buy' rating and a $41.19 mean price target suggesting 35-40% upside potential.
